english_query: If the road is icy, then driving is dangerous. The road is icy. Therefore, driving is dangerous. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says that if the road has ice, then driving is bad. Then, we look at the next part. It tells us the road has ice. Since the first part of the if-then thought is true, the second part must be true too. So, driving is bad. The last part says driving is bad. This is the same as what we found. So the last part follows for sure. follows

english_query: Propose a simple rule (e.g., a small penalty for anyone who contributes less than 3 tokens) and explain how it would affect the incentives.
ugf_response: First, we think about a game where people put things into a shared pile. If no one stops them, many will keep their things to save work. This is bad for the group. Second, we add a small hurt if a person puts in less than three things. This hurt makes the person feel bad. Third, this makes helping more good because the bad feeling is worse than the work of giving three things. People will change their plan to avoid the bad feeling. Next, some might say the bad feeling is too small to matter, but if it is bigger than the good from being slow, it works. So, the change pushes people to do the right thing for the group. The small bad feeling for low giving makes people put in more because they want to avoid the bad feeling. This helps the whole group get more from the shared pile.

english_query: what makes someone happy in the deepest way, not just for a moment
ugf_response:
Person 1: What makes a person feel good in a strong long way, not just for a short time?  

Person 2: It is when a person has good friends, a place they can call home, and work or play that they can see as real and true for them.  

Person 1: How do friends help make that long feeling of good?  

Person 2: Friends give love, help when we are sad, share laughs, and make us feel we are not alone. That feeling stays and can grow.  

Person 1: What about work or play? How do those add to a long good feeling?  

Person 2: Work or play that we can see as something we can do well gives us a sense that we can add to something. When we see a thing that comes after we do something, we feel a strong inside feeling and safe.  

Person 1: Is there a reason why feeling safe matters for this long good feeling?  

Person 2: When we feel safe we can think about other things, not just worry about something that can hurt. That lets us look at life and see more good.  

Person 1: Can a person feel this long good feeling if they do not have all those things at once?  

Person 2: Yes, a person can start with one small piece of work, like a friend or a small piece of work, and let that grow. Over time more parts can join and make the whole feel good for a long time.  

Person 1: So the main thing is to have many small parts that fit together and grow?  

Person 2: Yes, that is the idea. Small good parts can add up, and the more they fit, the more the feeling stays and gets stronger.

english_query: Also, the great old church teacher said true means being like the start. But god is not like a start. So god is not true.
ugf_response: Our mind is true when it is the same as the things it learns from. Things are true when they are the same as their maker, which is the mind of god. But this does not fit the truth of god, unless we talk about the Son, who has a start. If we talk about the truth of god in what he is, we must say he is like the start because he is not from another. So the truth of god can be called like the start, because his being is not different from his mind.

english_query: the question of what we owe to people we will never meet
ugf_response:
First, the first idea is a question about what we must do for people who do not live at the same time as us and who we will never see or know. This is a question about what we should give or do for people who are not here now but will be here after we are gone.

Second, the second idea is the part that follows from the first. It is the thought that if we think about these people, we might find that we have a thing that we must do for them. This means we might need to act in a way that makes the world good for them, even if they can never say thank you to us.

Third, the third idea is to look at whether this way of thinking is good. To see if this is true, we must look at how we decide what is right. If we only think about people we can see, we might make choices that hurt people who come after us. But if we try to think about everyone, it might be hard to know what to do. We must see if there is a good way to look at one thing and then look at another thing to see which is more important. We must see if the way we think about this is strong and if it stays the same when we look at different things.

english_query: If he turns on the light, then the room is bright. The room is not bright. Therefore, he does not turn on the light. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says if he turns on the light, then the room gets bright. This puts two things together. The first part is turning on the light. The second part is the room being bright. Next, we look at the second idea. It says the room is not bright. This tells us the second part did not happen. Now we think about what this means for the first part. If turning on the light always makes the room bright, and the room is not bright, then he could not have turned on the light. If he had turned it on, the room would be bright. But the room is not bright. So he did not turn it on. We can try to make a story where the first two ideas are true but the last one is not. In that story, he turns on the light, but the room is not bright. This breaks the first idea. So that story cannot happen. So this means the last idea must be true. The last thing we said follows for sure. follows

english_query: why saying something over and over does not make it true
ugf_response:
Person number one: Why do some people think that saying the same thing many times makes it true?

Person number two: It is a way the mind works. When we hear a thing many times, it feels easy to think about. Our minds like things that are easy to think about.

Person number one: But does being easy to think about mean the thing is real?

Person number two: No, it does not. Just because a thought comes to your head without work does not mean it is a true thing about the world.

Person number one: So if I say that the sky is green many times, will my mind start to believe it?

Person number two: Yes, it might. Your mind will start to feel that the green sky is a real thing because it is so easy to remember. But the sky is still blue.

Person number one: That sounds very bad. How can we stop our minds from being made to believe things that are not real?

Person number two: We must look at the world to see what is real. We must not only look at what is easy to think. We must look for things that show us what is true, even if those things are hard to think about.

english_query: It can not be said that God says yes to any bad thing. But if the mind is made by God, often God says yes to the bad love of a person, since often a child is made from bad love. So the mind is not made by God.
ugf_response: When a person does bad love, the part that is real is good; God says yes to that. But the part that is too much want is bad; God does not say yes to that.

english_query: Either the runner finished first or the runner finished second. The runner finished first. Therefore, the runner did not finish second. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says one of two things must be true: the runner was first or the runner was second. This is an either-or choice. Next, the second idea tells us that the runner was first. When we know one part of an either-or choice is true, the other part must not be true. Since the runner was first, it cannot be true that the runner was second. So, the last idea that the runner did not finish second fits with what we know. follows

english_query: How is that possible, old teacher, either in this case or any other thing--if while we are using the word the thing is going away in the change?
ugf_response: And what would you say of things like seeing and hearing, or any other kind of feeling? Is there any stopping in the act of seeing and hearing?

english_query: why being good at being a person is like being good at anything else
ugf_response:
Being good at being a person is a kind of doing well at a thing, just like being good at any other thing you try. First, when you try to be a good person you need to learn how to act, how to think, and how to feel in ways that help you and help others. You watch how other people act, you try to make like the parts that seem kind, and you see what works and what does not. When you see that a word you used made someone sad, you learn to change that word next time. This is the same as when you learn to play a game or to make food. You watch someone else do it, you try it yourself, you see if it goes well, and you try again. 

Second, you need to keep doing the thing over and over. Each time you try to be kind, to be telling the truth, to be calm, you get a little better because your mind and your heart get used to the new ways. This is the same as when you keep making a picture or keep making a pile. 

Third, you need help from other people. When a friend tells you that you made a good choice, you feel that the way you do things can be used again. When a friend tells you that you made a bad choice, you feel that you can change it. This is like a teacher who tells you when you are close to the point you want and when you are far. You use that talk to move your own things you do nearer to what you want. 

The next part, you need to know why you do the thing. You think about what you want for yourself and for the people around you. You think about what will make you feel safe and what will make others feel safe. This thinking is the same as when you think about why you want to win a game or why you want to finish a job. The thinking helps you pick the right moves. 

In all of these parts – watching, trying, getting help, and thinking – being good at being a person works the same way as being good at any other thing. The only not the same is the form of the thing you are working on. The form may be about how you act toward others, but the way you learn, do many times, and grow is the same as the way you learn, do many times, and grow at any other thing. So being good at being a person is not a something not known; it is just another kind of doing well by using the same steps that help you do well at anything else.

english_query: If the candidate wins the vote, then the candidate takes office. The candidate does not take office. Therefore, the candidate loses the vote. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea: if the person wins the pick, then they take the job. This means winning leads to taking the job. Next, we see the second idea: the person does not take the job. If winning always leads to taking the job, and the person did not take the job, then they could not have won. If they had won, they would be in the job. Since they are not in the job, they must not have won. Not winning is the same as losing the pick. So, the last idea follows for sure. follows

english_query: why people fear losing more than they enjoy winning the same amount
ugf_response:
People often feel more hurt when they lose something than they feel happy when they get that same thing. This is a strange way for our minds to work, but it happens to almost everyone. To understand this, we must look at how we see things we have and things we do not have.

First, imagine you have ten pieces of food to eat. If someone takes away two pieces, you will feel very sad. The feeling of things going away is very strong. Now, imagine you have nothing and someone gives you two pieces. You will feel happy, but that happy feeling is not as strong as the sad feeling you had when the food was taken away. The hurt from things going away is bigger than the happy feeling from things coming.

There are a few reasons why this happens. One reason is that we care more about what we already own. Once something is part of our life, it becomes part of who we are. When it goes away, it feels like a part of us is gone. When we get something new, it is nice, but it does not change who we are as much.

Another reason is how we stay safe. Long ago, when people lived in the wild, losing food or a place to sleep could mean death. Getting extra food was good, but losing the food you already had could be very bad for staying alive. Our minds grew to care more about not losing what we need to stay alive. This way of thinking stayed with us even though we do not live in the wild now.

Because of this, we often make choices that are not the best. We might stay in a bad place or keep a bad thing just because we are afraid of it going away. We let the fear of things going away stop us from trying to get something better. We are more afraid of the hurt of losing than we are excited by the chance to win.

english_query: If the environment changes so that V exceeds C, the pure Hawk strategy becomes an ESS because the benefit of winning outweighs the injury cost.
ugf_response: First we see that when the first thing (the good from getting the good thing) is more than the second thing (the bad from getting hurt) the good is bigger than the bad. Then we think that a way of always trying to win by fighting can stay as the best way because if you change to a quiet way you get less good and still may get hurt. But a different look is that if many always fight, many get hurt and the bad could add up, making fighting not the best. Yet if the first thing stays bigger than the second thing, even many fights still give more good than bad. So the most strong reply is that when the first thing is more than the second thing, always trying to win by fighting can stay as the best staying way. When the first thing is more than the second thing, always trying to win by fighting can be the best staying way. If the first thing is not more, then it is not the best way.

english_query: If the bridge is open, then traffic can cross the river. Traffic cannot cross the river. Therefore, the bridge is closed. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says if the bridge is open, then cars can go over the water. This is a tie between two things. The first part is the bridge being open. The second part is cars going over. Next, we look at the second idea. It says cars cannot go over the water. This tells us the second part did not happen. When the second part of an if-then tie does not happen, the first part could not have happened either. If the bridge were open, the cars would be going. But they are not. So the bridge cannot be open. If the bridge is not open, it is closed. This fits the last idea. We can try to break this. Imagine a story where the bridge is open but cars are stuck. The first idea says if open, then cars go. If cars do not go, the bridge must not be open. There is no way for the bridge to be open and for cars to not go, because the first idea stops that. So the last idea has to be true. It follows for sure. follows

english_query: why the rules should be the same for everyone
ugf_response:
The first thing we see is the starting point. This point says that all people are of the same kind when it comes to what is right and what is wrong. It says that no one person is better or more important than another when we think about how to live well. This is the first idea.

The second thing we see is what follows from the first idea. If all people are of the same kind, then the way we tell people how to act must be the same for every person. We cannot have one set of ways for one group and a different set of ways for another group. This is the second idea.

The third thing we see is whether the way they think is good. To know if the thinking is good, we must look at how the first idea leads to the second idea. If it is true that all people are of the same kind, then it must be true that the ways of living must be the same. The path from the first idea to the second idea is straight and clear. If the first idea is true, then the second idea must also be true. However, if the first idea is not true, then the second idea might not be true either. The power of the whole thing rests on the first idea.

english_query: If the engine has fuel, then the car can move. If the car can move, then the door can be opened from outside. Therefore, if the engine has fuel, then the door can be opened from outside. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we see that if the engine has the stuff it burns to run, the car can move. Then, we see that if the car can move, the door can be opened from the outside. We join these ideas. If the engine has the stuff, the car moves. If the car moves, the door opens. So, if the engine has the stuff, the door opens. The last idea says this. So it follows for sure. follows

english_query: how to think about what the other person will do before you act
ugf_response: First, you must stop and look at the person you want to act with. Second, you must think about what that person wants or what makes them feel good. Third, you must think about what you want to do. The thing after the third thing is to think about how what you do will change the things that the other person wants. The thing after that is to think about how the other person will see what you do and how they will feel about it. The thing after that is to think about what the other person will do after they see your act. The thing after that is to see if the thing you do will lead to a good end for both of you or if it will lead to a bad end. The thing after that is to change your own plan if you see that your plan will make the other person do something that is bad for you or bad for the whole group. Finally, you can move forward with your act when you feel that you know what will happen next.

english_query: Also, things that do not need each other do not seem to have one way to count their time; for there seems to be one time for all things that happen in time; since the first moving thing, which time counts, is in some way the cause of all moving things. But things that last for ever do not need each other, for one spirit is not the cause of another spirit. So there is not just one way to count their time.
ugf_response: For things to be counted by one thing, it is not needed that this one thing is the cause of all, but that it is simpler than the rest.

english_query: why people sometimes pick the worse thing even when they know better
ugf_response: First, we must look at how a person thinks when they want to make a choice. We can see that the brain is like a machine that wants to save its power. Making a hard choice takes much work and much power from the brain. Because of this, the brain often looks for a way to be fast and easy. Second, we see that the brain likes things that feel good right now. A person might know that a certain thing will be good for them in a long time, but they also see a thing that feels good this very second. The brain often picks the thing that gives a quick feeling of being very happy because it is easy to do. Third, we must look at how people see things. Sometimes, the brain does not see the whole truth. The brain might only see a small part of what is happening. This makes the person think they are making a good choice, even when they are not. The brain also holds onto old ways of thinking that might not be true anymore. The step after the third thing is when we see that people are often afraid. If a person feels fear, they might not be able to think clearly. Fear makes the brain want to run or hide, which stops the brain from thinking about what is really best for the long time. Finally, we see that when many people do the same thing, a person might do it too, even if it is a bad thing. This happens because the brain thinks that if everyone else is doing it, it must be okay. This is how people end up picking the thing that is not good, even when they know the truth.

english_query: Also, water is by its way of being wet and moving, and wet moving things cannot stay on a round ball, as we can see. So, because the sky is a round ball, there cannot be water above it.
ugf_response: The answer is clear from what was said before, for the last two ways of seeing it. But for the first way, an old church teacher gives two answers. First, he says that a thing that looks like a cup from the bottom does not have to be round out on the top. Second, he says that the water above the sky is not wet and moving, but is hard like ice, sitting outside it in a hard state, which some writers call the glass sky.

english_query: Also, body things are not ready to be known, but are known by seeing and imagining, which the good spirits do not have. So the good spirits do not know body things.
ugf_response: If a good spirit got his knowing of body things from the body things themselves, he would need to make them ready to be known by taking away their body parts. But he does not get his knowing of them from the body things themselves; he knows body things by clear marks of things that fit him well; just as our mind knows by marks it makes clear by taking away body parts.

english_query: Yes, that also stays the same.
ugf_response: And a good life is the same as a right and good life. Does that also stay good?

What! because the well known teacher of speaking did not want to say no that the speaker knew what is right and good, and said that to anyone who came to him not knowing them he could teach them, and then from this saying there came a fight-the thing which you love very much, and to which not he, but you, brought the talk by your hard questions-(do you really think there is any truth in all this?) They think these are good things, even though I do not hate those who get them or those who have them. ugf_response: Do you think, old teacher, that a man who cannot stand up for himself is in a good spot?

english_query: Also, the great old teacher said that doing things comes before the power to do them. But getting the idea is a doing that is not the same as other doings of the mind. An old man who can think well from far away said that the first move is called getting the idea; that getting the idea about a certain thing is called wanting to know; that which stays and fits the soul to what is known is called finding it; and finding it when it stays in the same man, looking at and checking itself, is called being good at making right calls; and being good at making right calls if it grows makes thought, which is right talk inside; from which, they say, comes talk said with the tongue. So it seems that getting the idea is a special power.
ugf_response: All those doings that the old man who can think well from far away tells us about belong to one power, which is the mind power. For this power first just sees something; and this doing is called getting the idea. Second, it points what it sees to knowing something else, or to some work; and this is called wanting to know. And when it goes on to look for what it wants to know, it is called finding it. When, by looking at something known for sure, it checks what it found, it is said to know or to be good at making right calls, which belongs to being good at making right calls; for it is for the man who is good at making right calls to check, as the great old teacher said. And when once it has something for sure, as being checked all the way, it thinks about the way to make it known to others; and this is the ordering of talk inside, from which comes talk outside. For every different doing does not make the powers change, but only what cannot be put back to the one same start, as we said before.

So many things are joined in the one way of the thing, which the higher power looks at by itself; while they are not the same in the ways looked at by the lower powers by themselves. english_query: how we build a picture of the world from small pieces of what we see
ugf_response: First, we use our eyes to see small parts of things around us. We see a red shape and a round shape. Then our mind puts these small parts together. It makes a whole picture in our head. This picture is not the real thing but it is close to it. We do this all the time. When we see a long nose and big ears, we think of a big gray animal with a long nose. We build a big idea from many small sights. This helps us know what is happening without looking at every tiny part. It saves us time and lets us move through the world. But sometimes our mind puts pieces together in a way that is not right. Then our picture is not true. So we must check our picture against what we see next. If new pieces do not fit, we change our picture. This way we keep our view of the world close to the real world.

english_query: why what you already believe changes how you see new things
ugf_response:
Person 1: I ask why what I already think changes how I see new things.  

Person 2: When you think something, that thought stays in your head. It works like a way you look at all new things. It can make you see them in a way that go with the thought.  

Person 1: Can you give a simple show of that?  

Person 2: Imagine you put a dark cover over what you look at. The cover blocks some of the light, so everything looks a bit dark. Your thought works like that cover.  

Person 1: Does that mean I can change the way I see by changing my thoughts?  

Person 2: Yes. If you try to think new ways, the old thought will be less big. Then the new things will look more easy.  

Person 1: How can I make my old thought less big?  

Person 2: You can ask yourself if the old thought go with the new thing. You can look for other thoughts that also go with it. You can try looking at the new thing without the old thought first.

ugf_response:
Person 1: I have a question about why people do not work together when they could.

Person 2: That is a good question. It happens because each person thinks about what is best for them right now.

Person 1: But if they work together, do they not both get more?

Person 2: Yes, they do. If they both help each other, they both win. But if one helps and the other does not, the one who helps loses.

Person 1: So they are scared of losing?

Person 2: Exactly. They are afraid the other person will not help. So they choose not to help either.

Person 1: That means they both get less than they could have.

Person 2: Yes. They miss out on the good thing because they do not trust each other.

Person 1: How do they fix that?

Person 2: They need to talk and promise to do the right thing. Then they can trust and win together.

english_query: why a true belief for bad reasons is not the same as knowing
ugf_response:
Person 1: I hear people say that having a real thought that fits how things are, but for a wrong cause, is not the same as to know. Why is that?  
Person 2: When we say we know something, we need two things: a real thought that fits the way things are, and a good cause that leads us to that thought.  
Person 1: So the thought being real is only one part?  
Person 2: Yes, the thought must be real, but if the cause is wrong the thought is still not full know. The good cause shows that we got the thought in a way that usually gives true thoughts.  
Person 1: What do you mean by a good cause?  
Person 2: A good cause is a reason that fits with what we see or with a way that usually gives true thoughts. It is not just a lucky guess or a not true idea that happens to be right.  
Person 1: If the cause is wrong, does that mean the thought could be right by chance?  
Person 2: Exactly. The thought can be right, but because the cause is wrong we cannot say we really know it. We only have a true thought for a bad cause.  
Person 1: So knowing needs both a true thought and a good cause together?  
Person 2: Yes, both parts together make know. One part without the other is not enough.  
Person 1: I see now why a true thought for bad reasons is not the same as knowing. Thank you.

english_query: In the quiet cloister of Saint Aurelia's Monastery, the library garden doubles as an observatory. Brother Caelum, a monk who charts the motions of the stars, debates with Sister Mirabel, who teaches novices how to assess testimony in the abbey's tribunal.
ugf_response: First, we see that the two people are talking about how to know if something that someone says is true. Then, we see that the first person uses his own look at the sky to make a group of points about how the lights move. Next, we see that the second person helps new ones learn to check what other people say in the place where people decide about right and wrong. The thing that could be bad is that a wrong thing could be used to make a bad choice in the place where people decide about right and wrong or to think the sky moves a wrong way. One strong thought is that when we can see the sky, the signs we see are the best help. Another strong thought is that when we cannot see, a good word from a person we think is good can be used. Some might say that the eye can be made to see wrong by clouds or other things, so we should not trust the look alone. Others might say that a word can be made up, so we should not trust the word alone. So we need a way that uses both the look and the word, giving more say to the look when it is clear. That seems the best way. The best answer is to use what we see in the sky when we can, but also to check the word from others, and to keep both in mind when we decide.

english_query: I can't just toss away the work we've done. Every failed test brings us closer to the breakthrough. The council's catalyst is a shortcut, but it feels like admitting defeat.
ugf_response: First, we look at the idea that we should not throw away the work we have done. This makes sense because time and effort are hard to get back. Then, we see that every time a try fails we learn something that helps us get to the big win. This is a good way to think about hard jobs. But then we hear about the way the group offers. It is a fast path. A fast path means we get there faster but maybe we do not learn as much. The person feels like using the fast path is like saying I give up. This is a strong feeling. We must ask if learning is more important than finishing. If the point is just to finish the fast path is good. If the point is to understand and be strong for next time the long way is better. Someone might say the fast path saves time and money which is also good. But if we use the fast path we might not be ready for the next hard thing. The feeling of losing comes from not trusting our own hard work. So the choice is between going fast and being strong. If we want to be really ready we should keep going the hard way. The fast path feels wrong because it leaves out the part where we grow. We should not use the fast path because the hard work makes us stronger and ready for the future even if it takes longer.

english_query: the question of what we owe to people we will never meet
ugf_response:
Person number one: I have a thought that makes my head hurt. Do we have to care about people we will never see?

Person number two: That is a very big thought. Many people think we do.

Person number one: But why? If I do not know them, and they do not know me, why should I do things to help them?

Person number two: Think about it this way. If we only care about the people we see, the world will become a very bad place. We must think about the people who will live after we are gone.

Person number one: So you mean people who are not born yet? How can I care about someone who is not here yet?

Person number two: You care by making sure the world stays good for them. If you take everything now and leave nothing, you are being bad to them. You are making their life hard before they even start.

Person number one: I see. So it is like when we use a thing and we must make sure it is still good for the next person who wants to use it.

Person number two: Yes. That is a good way to say it. We have a thing we must do to leave the world in a state where others can live well. Even if they are far away or not born yet, their lives matter.

english_query: why what you already believe changes how you see new things
ugf_response:
Person 1: I have a question for you. Why do people see new things in different ways?

Person 2: That is a good question. It happens because of what we already know and believe. We use our old ideas to help us understand new ones.

Person 1: So if I believe something is true, I will only see things that go with that idea?

Person 2: Yes, that is often what happens. Your mind looks for signs that are the same as what you think is right. It is easier for the mind to find things that agree with you.

Person 1: What about things that do not go with my idea? Do we just ignore them?

Person 2: We might try to ignore them. Or we might think they are wrong. It is hard to change what we believe. So we stick to what we know.

Person 1: That sounds like a bad spot to be in. How can we get out of it?

Person 2: You have to try hard to look at the other side. You must ask if you could be wrong. Then you can see the whole picture, not just the part you like.

english_query: It looks like god is not outside of time. For we should not say things made about god. The old teacher who thought well says the now that moves makes time, but the now that stays still makes being outside of time. The great old church teacher says god is the one who makes being outside of time. So god is not outside of time.
ugf_response: The now that stays still is said to make being outside of time for how we see it. Just as our seeing of time comes from seeing the now move, our seeing of being outside of time comes from seeing the now stay still. When the great old church teacher says god is the one who makes being outside of time, he means the kind of being outside of time that things get from him. For god gives his being outside of time to some things in the same way he gives his not changing.

english_query: Dean Marlowe chuckles, "Ah, you think I'm just being modest? In truth, I always know where the credit belongs. Perhaps you should look more closely at how leaders talk about success and failure." The player receives a hint highlighting the contrast between internal and external attributions.

Some people say it is because of them. They look inside. Others say it is because of other things. They look outside. The man acts like he looks outside. He does not take the good parts for himself. He wants to seem small so people trust him. This is a way to keep power. Someone might say he is really nice. But the laugh says no. He is good at seeing how people see him. So, he uses outside words to look good. The man uses outside words for good things to look nice and get people to like him, which is a plan to stay in control.

english_query: Also the great old church teacher says that men who use bad spirits do big things by talks just for them; good god people do them by the right way for all; bad god people do them by signs of the right way for all. But men who use bad spirits do big things because the bad spirits hear them. So the bad spirits can do big things. So much more can the good spirits.

So when something is done outside the normal way by a power we do not know, we call it a big thing for us. So when bad spirits do things with their own power, we call them big things only for us, not in truth. This is how men who use bad spirits do big things through bad spirits; these are called talks just for them, because the power of a made thing is like the power of one person in a town. So when a man who uses bad spirits does something by talk with the bad one, it is like a talk just for him. On the other side, the right way of god is in all things like the law of the town. So good god people, who do big things by the right way of god, are said to do them by the right way for all: but bad god people by the signs of the right way for all, like saying the name of the one god became man, or using other signs to do with god.

english_query: why the rules should be the same for everyone
ugf_response:
We see a group of people living together in a place. To live well, they make a set of ways they must act. These ways are meant to keep things good and safe for all. The big question is why these ways should be the very same for every person. Some might say that if one person can do something that others cannot, then the ways are not good for all. If the ways are not the same, then some people will have more power or more things than others just because of who they are. This can lead to a place where some people are hurt and others are not.

Some people might say that the ways must be the same for everyone to keep things simple and easy to see. If every person follows the same way, then no one can say that the way is bad for them alone. It makes it easy to know what is right and what is wrong. This helps everyone feel that they are part of the same group. When the ways are the same, it stops people from being mean to others by using their power to make different ways for different people.

Other people might think that the ways should not be the very same for everyone in every case. They might say that some people have things that are very different. For one thing, one person might be very strong and another might not have much power in their body. They might say that to make things good for everyone, we must look at what each person needs. If we use the same way for a person who is very strong and a person who has no power in their body, the person with no power in their body might end up with much less than the strong person. These people think that making things good means looking at the person, not just the way.

In the end, we are left with a hard choice. We must choose between making the ways the same for all to keep things good in a simple way, or making the ways different to help each person in their own way. Both ways want to make a good place, but they see the way to do it in different ways. One way looks at the whole group as one, while the other way looks at each person as a single thing.

english_query: Either the suspect was at home or the suspect was at work. The suspect was not at home. Therefore, the suspect was at work. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, the first idea says that one of two things must be true: the person was at home, or the person was at work. Then, the second idea tells us that the person was not at home. Since the first idea says one of those two places had to be where the person was, and we know it was not the home, it must be the other place. So, the last idea that the person was at work follows for sure. follows

english_query: If the engine has fuel, then the car can move. The car cannot move. Therefore, the engine has no fuel. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says if the engine has the stuff that makes it go, then the car can move. Next, we see the second idea. It says the car cannot move. We need to see if this means the engine has no stuff that makes it go. If the engine had the stuff that makes it go, the car would move. But the car is not moving. So the engine could not have had the stuff that makes it go. If it did, the car would be moving. Since the car is not moving, the first part must not be true. So the engine does not have the stuff that makes it go. This last idea follows for sure from the first two. follows

english_query: Also, some men who think well said that the best good for a man is in knowing things that stand alone. So if the soul, when it is not with the body, can know these things, its good would be full just by being not with the body; which is not right to say.
ugf_response: The best good for a man is not in knowing any things that stand alone; but in knowing God, who is seen only by help from above. Knowing other things that stand alone, if known in a full way, gives much good--but not the final and best good. But the soul, when not with the body, does not know them in a full way, as was shown before in this talk.

english_query: If the river is high, then the bank is flooded. If the bank is flooded, then the bank is flooded. Therefore, if the river is high, then the bank is flooded. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.

The second idea does not add any new join, it just repeats that a bank with a lot of water stays with a lot of water. The last idea is exactly the same as the first idea. Because the first idea is given as true, the last idea must also be true. Nothing can make the first idea not true. So anyone can agree with both earlier ideas and still have to agree that if the river is high then the bank has a lot of water. So the last idea follows for sure. follows

english_query: Also, power is the main idea of how things work. But the power of god is what god really is, because there is no extra in god; and for what god really is there is no main idea. So there is no power in god.

So in god the thought of power is kept because it is the main idea of an end; but not as the main idea of doing, because doing is what god really is; maybe after our way of thinking, because what god really is, which already has all good parts of made things, can be thought of as a doing or as a power; also it can be thought of as a thing that has a kind, and as a kind. So the thought of power is kept in god as long as it is the main idea of an end.

english_query: If the door is unlocked, then the door can be opened from outside. The door is locked. Therefore, the door cannot be opened from outside. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.

The first idea only tells us what happens when the door is not locked; it says nothing about what happens when it is locked. Because the door being locked does not stop the door from maybe being opened, we can not be sure that the door can not be opened from outside. So the last part does not follow for sure. does not follow

english_query: No, not to a man able to think well, as the talk shows: do you think that all our worry should go to making life last as long as it can, and to learning those things we do to keep us safe from bad things always; like that thing of talking well that saves men in places where laws are said, and which you tell me to learn?
ugf_response: Yes, for real, and very good talk too.

english_query: how to think about what could happen and how likely it is
ugf_response: First, we look at the things that might happen in the future. We think about all the different ways things could go. Then, we try to guess how often each of those ways might come true. Some things happen very often, like the sun coming up in the morning. We know this will happen, so we say it is a sure thing. Other things happen only a few times, like finding money on the ground. We know this is not a sure thing. When we think about what could happen, we do not just write down every way it could go. We also think about how much we believe each one will happen. This helps us make better choices. If we know something is very much going to happen, we get ready for it. If we know something is not going to happen much, we might not worry as much. This way of thinking helps us see the world more clearly. It lets us plan for the things that are most real and not spend too much time on things that are just dreams. We use this idea every day when we decide to carry a rain cover or stay home. We look at the sky and think about rain. If the sky is dark, we think rain is a sure thing. If the sky is bright, we think rain is not a sure thing. This simple thinking helps us live our lives in a good way.

english_query: If the candidate wins the vote, then the candidate takes office. The candidate does not take office. Therefore, the candidate loses the vote. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea: if the person wins the pick, then they get the job. Next, we see the second idea: the person does not get the job. If winning the pick meant they would get the job, and they did not get the job, then they could not have won the pick. It would be wrong to say they won if they did not get the job. So, since they did not get the job, we know for sure they did not win the pick. Not winning is the same as losing the pick. So this means the last idea follows for sure. follows

english_query: the problem with thinking only two choices exist when there are more
ugf_response:
The first idea is that a person sometimes thinks there are only two ways for something to be. They think it is either this way or that way, and nothing else can happen. This is a way of thinking that can be wrong because many times there are many more ways for things to go.

The second idea is what comes from this way of thinking. If a person believes there are only two ways, they will make a choice between those two ways. Because they do not see the other ways that could work, they might pick a way that is not good. They might pick a way that makes things worse when a better way was there all along.

The third idea is whether this way of thinking is good or bad. This way of thinking is bad. It is not a good way to use your mind. When you only see two ways, you are not seeing the whole truth. You are missing the other ways that are real. To think well, you must look for all the ways things could be, not just the two ways you see right now. If you do not do this, your choices will not be good.

english_query: If the book is on the shelf, then she can borrow the book. If she can borrow the book, then driving is dangerous. Therefore, if the book is on the shelf, then driving is dangerous. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says if the book is on the high place, then she can take it. Next, we look at the second idea. It says if she can take the book, then driving is bad. We can put these two ideas together. If the book being on the high place leads to her taking it, and her taking it leads to driving being bad, then the book being on the high place leads to driving being bad. So the last idea follows for sure from the first two ideas. follows

english_query: how doing good things over and over makes you into a good person
ugf_response:
We see many people who try to help, who give food, who share time, who speak kind words. They do these good things many times, over and over, and they notice that they feel a warm feeling inside. This makes them think about a hard question: does doing good things again and again turn a person into a good person? The question is not easy because it asks what it means to be a good person in the first place.

One group of people say that when a person does good things a lot, the act becomes a way of doing something that you do often. They say the way is like a part of you that gets more strong each time you use it. In this view, the more you help, the more you become a kind and nice person, because the way shapes the way you see the world and the way you act without thinking. They point to the fact that many who keep helping also talk about feeling less only thinking about yourself and more caring, and they think that the feeling shows the change inside.

Another group of people say that the reason you do good may be the key. They ask if you help because you want to look good, because you hope to get something you get back, or because you fear being called bad. If the reason is to get something you get, they say, the thing may look good on the outside but the inside may still be only thinking about yourself. They look at cases where a person helps a lot but later shows mean or makes others feel bad, and they say this shows that the outside things do not always change the inside you.

A third group says that what matters most is how you think about the world. They say that if you think that helping is the right thing, then each good thing is a sign that you are moving toward a right way of seeing things. But they also say you could think the right way without ever doing a single good thing, and still feel that you are on a good path. For them, the inside thought and the outside thing are both parts of the picture, and one does not the same as the other. They say that a person can act kind because it is an idea they follow, but still feel empty inside, while another person can feel kind inside and only sometimes act on it.

All these ideas have reasons that make sense. The way view shows how repeated doing can shape a way of doing. The reason view shows how the hidden want behind a thing can stay the same even if the thing looks good. The thought view shows how the way you see the world can stay not the same from what you do. Because each of these reasons can be true in different cases, the hard question stays open. We can keep looking at many cases, talk with many people, and try to feel inside what feels right, but we may still not have a single answer that works for every person. The question stays a big thought for anyone who wonders how doing good many times can change who they are.

english_query: If the bread has risen, then the yeast was alive. The bread has risen. Therefore, the yeast was alive. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says that if the soft food has gone up, then the small living things were alive. This is a plan about what must be true if the food goes up. Next, we look at the second idea. It tells us a fact about the world right now. The soft food has gone up. Now we put these two ideas together. Since the plan says the small living things must be alive when the food goes up, and we know the food did go up, we can see what must be true. The first part of the if-then has happened. So the second part of the if-then must also be true. That means the small living things were alive. We can check this by trying to find a way for the first two ideas to be true but the last one to be wrong. Could the food go up and the plan be true, but the small living things were not alive? No, because the plan says they must be alive if the food goes up. So there is no way for the last idea to be wrong if the first two are right. This means the last idea follows for sure. follows

english_query: Also, all knowing is either the start of the thing known, or started by it. But the knowing of god is not the start of bad things, nor is it started by bad things. So god does not know bad things.
ugf_response: The knowing of god is not the start of bad things; but it is the start of the good way in which bad things are known.

english_query: why being sure about something does not make it right
ugf_response: First, we think about the feeling of being sure. This feeling happens in a person when they believe a thing is true and have no part of them that thinks it might not be true. Second, we must look at how we know things. We often know things because of what we see or what we think we see. Third, we must see that our eyes and our minds can be wrong. Sometimes our eyes see something that is not there, or our minds do something that is not the right way to think. The thing after the third thing, we must see that having a strong feeling in our hearts does not change the world outside of us. If a person feels very sure that the sun moves around the earth, that feeling is strong, but the earth still moves around the sun. The thing after the thing after the third thing, we see that being sure is a feeling inside a person, while being right is a way that a thing is in the real world. Because a feeling and the real world are two different things, the feeling cannot make the real world change. Finally, we see that a person can be very sure and be wrong at the very same time.

But the first man, while in the good place, had a life of great good, as an old man who knows much says. And the great old church teacher says: 'If man had the same likes as now, how good must he have been in that place of great good!' So the first man in the good place saw what god really is.
ugf_response: Man had good in the good place, but not that perfect good he was meant for, which comes from seeing what god really is. He had, however, 'a life of good in some way,' as the great old church teacher says, in so far as he was whole and right.

english_query: Yes certainly, if he wanted to be so.
ugf_response: But would he not have wanted? He would, for sure, have wanted to make his own son a good man and a good person; he could not have been mad at him, or have on set out to keep from giving to him his own good way. Did you never hear that he made his son a well known rider of horses; and had him taught to stand straight on the horse and throw a sharp stick, and to do many other great things; and in anything which could be learned from a teacher he was well trained? Have you not heard from our old people of him?

english_query: how to think about what is fair when people start in different places
ugf_response: First, we must look at the way people start their lives. Some people start with much more than others. They may have more money, more food, or more help from their families. Other people start with very little and must work much harder just to get what they need to live. Second, we must ask what it means for things to be right for everyone. If we say that everyone must have the same thing at the end, we might not see that some people had a much harder road to get there. Third, we can look at it in a different way. We can say that things are right if we make sure that the people who start with nothing get the help they need to reach a good place. This means we do not just look at where people end up, but we look at the way they get there. The step after the third step is to think about how much help a person needs to have the same chances as someone who started with much. If the person with much has a head start, then the person with little needs more help to make the run of life right. The step after that is to say that a good way to think about this is to make sure that the starting point does not stop a person from reaching their best life. We want to make sure that the things a person is born with do not decide everything that happens to them later.

english_query: If she takes the medicine, then her headache goes away. She does not take the medicine. Therefore, her headache does not go away. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea given to us. It says that if she takes the special drink, then her head pain goes away. This is a promise about what happens when she takes the drink. It does not say what happens if she does not take the drink. Next, we look at the second idea. It tells us that she does not take the special drink. Now we must see if the last idea follows for sure. The last idea says her head pain does not go away. But wait, the first idea only tells us what happens when she takes the drink. It does not say that taking the drink is the only way to stop the pain. Maybe her head pain goes away on its own. Maybe she drinks water instead. So, it is possible that she does not take the special drink, but her head pain still goes away. Because we can imagine a story where she does not take the drink but her head feels fine, the last idea does not follow for sure from the first two. does not follow

english_query: Some researchers argue that loss aversion is an adaptive heuristic: in ancestral environments, a loss (e.g., a missed hunt) could be fatal, while a comparable gain was less critical. From that perspective, λ≈2.25 may be rational rather than a bias.
ugf_response: First, we look at the idea that being afraid of losing things helps a person stay alive. In the old days, if a person did not find food, they might die. If they found extra food, they were just a bit happier. So, caring more about not losing what you have makes sense for staying alive. This means the number 2.25, which shows how much more we hate losing than we like getting more, could be a good choice. But we must ask if this is still a good choice today. Today, most people do not die if they miss a small chance to buy something or if they lose a small piece of money. The bad thing that might happen is not death, but maybe just having less. Also, if we are too afraid of losing, we might miss good chances to get more. For one case, if we are too scared to try a new job because we might lose the old one, we stay stuck. This fear can stop us from doing well. Some might say that being careful is always good. But being too careful can be bad if it stops us from growing. The old world was very different from now. What helped our long ago family members live might hurt us now if we do not change. We need to see if the fear fits the real bad thing that might happen. If the bad thing is small, the big fear is not a good way to think. It leads to bad choices, like keeping bad things just because they are ours. So, while it made sense long ago, it is not always the best way to think now. Being afraid of losing things was a good way to stay alive for our long ago family members because losing could mean death. But today, this fear is often too strong and can stop us from making good choices, so it acts more like a wrong step than a good way to do things.

english_query: If the meeting starts at noon, then everyone arrives before noon. If everyone arrives before noon, then the ground is wet. Therefore, if the meeting starts at noon, then the ground is wet. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we see the first idea says if the meeting starts at the middle of the day, then everyone gets there before that time. Then, the second idea says if everyone gets there before that time, then the dirt is wet. We put these two thoughts together. If the meeting starts at the middle of the day, everyone gets there early. If everyone gets there early, the dirt is wet. So if the meeting starts at the middle of the day, the dirt is wet. The last idea follows for sure. follows

english_query: In the underground mining colony of Vespera, the player oversees the allocation of resources for a new deep‑drill project.
ugf_response: First, we look at the place under the ground where people live. The player decides how to use what they have. This is a big job because there is not enough of everything for everyone. Then, we think about the new thing that goes very deep into the earth. It might find new things, like clean water or power. But it also takes a lot of time and food to use. We must ask if this is a good use of what we have. If we use all our food and power on this deep thing, the people might get sick or cold. That would be bad. So, the player must be careful. We should check if this deep thing is really needed. Maybe there is a better way to get what we need. Or maybe we can wait. But if the people are running out of air or power, then this deep thing is very important. We have to think about the bad things that might happen. If we fail, we might lose everything. If we do well, we might save the group. This is a hard choice. Some might say we should always try to grow. Others might say we should stay safe. I think keeping people alive comes first when you are under the ground. If you die, you cannot grow. So, we must make sure the people are safe before we spend on this deep thing. The player should only start the deep thing if the group is in trouble of running out of needs for life, and only if there is a good chance it will work without hurting the people now.

Then, the next line of words says the visitor is from the north. Since this is true, the part about the south must be not true. So the last line of words says the visitor is not from the south. This last idea follows for sure from the first ideas. follows

english_query: Also, if you say the feeling part in man does not end; on the other hand, the old teacher who thinks well says that things that end and things that do not end are very different kinds. But the feeling part in a horse, a big cat, and other animals that cannot talk does end. So, if in man it does not end, the feeling part in man and in these animals is not the same kind. Now we call a living thing an animal because it has a feeling part; so 'animal' would not be one kind for man and other animals, which is wrong.
ugf_response: It is not the shapes, but the whole things put together, that are put into kinds. Now man ends like other animals. So the change between ending and not ending, which is about the shapes, does not mean man and other animals are of different kinds.
